sources.notes: Choose directions for rabbits placed in every 0-cell so no rabbit jumps.
Official editorial reduces the obstruction to isolated zero-runs between flowers.

annotation.summary: Given a binary line of flowers and empty pots, orient rabbits placed at every empty pot so every rabbit is blocked from moving.
annotation.constraints: Total n is 2e5, so a single scan over zero-runs is enough.
annotation.core_idea: A run of at least two rabbits can orient inward so every rabbit is blocked by another rabbit, and a boundary single rabbit can look outside the line. The only impossible local shape is a single zero between two ones: that rabbit must look at a flower on either side and no opposite rabbit can block it. Thus check whether substring 101 occurs.
tricks: only isolated internal zero is bad

solution_variants.name: zero-run-scan
solution_variants.summary: Scan all zero-runs and reject exactly a length-one run with a flower on both sides.

tags.evidence: The accepted solution is a direct local-pattern scan with careful boundary cases.
The YES proof explicitly orients each feasible zero-run inward or outward.
The condition is naturally expressed in terms of lengths and boundaries of zero-runs.
